chapter: Chapter 3: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

questions.question: Why does a₁/a₂ ≠ b₁/b₂ guarantee a unique solution?
questions.answer: Because the lines intersect at exactly one point.

questions.question: Why do parallel lines have no solution?
questions.answer: Because they never intersect.

questions.question: Why do coincident lines have infinitely many solutions?
questions.answer: Because every point on one line lies on the other.

questions.question: Why is a dependent system always consistent?
questions.answer: Because it has infinitely many solutions.

questions.question: Why is graphical method not exact?
questions.answer: Because solutions depend on scale and visual accuracy.

questions.question: Why are algebraic methods preferred over graphical method?
questions.answer: Because they provide exact solutions.

questions.question: Why must equations be converted to standard form for cross-multiplication?
questions.answer: Because the method works only for ax + by + c = 0 form.

questions.question: Why does elimination method require equal coefficients?
questions.answer: To eliminate one variable easily.

questions.question: Why is substitution method easier when a coefficient is 1?
questions.answer: Because substitution becomes simple.

questions.question: Why can a pair of linear equations never have exactly two solutions?
questions.answer: Because two straight lines cannot intersect at two points.

questions.question: Why does the solution satisfy both equations?
questions.answer: Because it lies on both lines.

questions.question: Why are real-life problems converted into equations?
questions.answer: To find unknown quantities mathematically.

questions.question: Why do proportional equations represent the same line?
questions.answer: Because one equation is a multiple of the other.

questions.question: Why does changing coefficients change the position of a line?
questions.answer: Because slope and intercept depend on coefficients.

questions.question: Why does same slope imply parallel lines?
questions.answer: Because both lines rise or fall at the same rate.

questions.question: Why does different slope ensure intersection?
questions.answer: Because lines cross each other at some point.

questions.question: Why is an independent system important in applications?
questions.answer: Because it gives a unique solution.

questions.question: Why is an inconsistent system not useful in real-life problems?
questions.answer: Because it has no valid solution.

questions.question: Why does graphical intersection represent equality of equations?
questions.answer: Because both equations have the same values at that point.

questions.question: Why is linear modelling important in daily life?
questions.answer: Because many situations involve constant rate of change.

questions.question: Why are two equations required to find two unknowns?
questions.answer: Because one equation is insufficient to determine both values.

questions.question: Why do equations need to be solved simultaneously?
questions.answer: Because the solution must satisfy both equations.

questions.question: Why does cross-multiplication fail for parallel lines?
questions.answer: Because the determinant becomes zero.

questions.question: Why does linear graph always form a straight line?
questions.answer: Because the highest power of the variable is one.

questions.question: Why are pair of linear equations called simultaneous equations?
questions.answer: Because they are solved together at the same time.
